DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF SUSAN WILLIAMS MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS AND FOR SANCTIONS - THE MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS IS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS. DEFENDANTS' LATE-FILED OPPOSITION THAT EXCEEDS THE PAGE LIMIT LACKS DOCUMENTARY EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE OPPOSITION. THE COURT DOES NOT FIND THAT A DEMAND LETTER WRITTEN BY COUNSEL AS PRIVILEGED SETTLEMENT COMMUNICATIONS, IS SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE OPPOSITION. HOWEVER, THE DECLARATION OF HERMANSON PROVIDES SOME EVIDENCE FOR THE NEED FOR THE DOCUMENTS. SEE HERMANSON DECL., 3:12-23. THE SUBPOENAS ARE LIMITED AS FOLLOWS: TWO YEARS PRIOR TO THE EVENT TO THE PRESENT. PERSONAL RECORDS SHALL BE LIMITED TO EARNINGS, WITHOUT TAX INFORMATION. THE PARTIES SHALL ENTER INTO A STANDARD PROTECTIVE ORDER BEFORE THE RECORDS ARE PRODUCED. ALL REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: PETER CATALANOTTI; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROSS L. LIBENSON OF THE LIBENSON LAW, TEL. NO. 1-510-451-4441 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SUSAN WILLIAMS Motion To Quash Subpoenas And For Sanctions - The Motion to Quash subpoenas is GRANTED and DENIED as follows. Defendants' late-filed Opposition that exceeds the page limit lacks documentary evidence to support the Opposition. The Court does not find that a demand letter written by counsel as privileged settlement communications, is sufficient evidence to support the Opposition. However, the declaration of Hermanson provides some evidence for the need for the documents. See Hermanson Decl., 3:12-23. The subpoenas are limited as follows: two years prior to the event to the present. Personal records shall be limited to earnings, without tax information. The parties shall enter into a standard protective order before the records are produced. All request for sanctions are denied. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro-Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt
